_your native open source AI agent — desktop app, CLI, and API — for code, workflows, and everything in between_

 
  
  
  
 
 
 
 

goose is a general-purpose AI agent that runs on your machine. Not just for code — use it for research, writing, automation, data analysis, or anything you need to get done.
...
A native desktop app for macOS, Linux, and Windows. A full CLI for terminal workflows. An API to embed it anywhere. Built in Rust for performance and portability.
...
goose works with 15+ providers — Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Ollama, OpenRouter, Azure, Bedrock, and more. Use API keys or your existing Claude, ChatGPT, or Gemini subscriptions via [ACP](https://goose-docs.ai/docs/guides/acp-providers). Connect to 70+ extensions via the [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/) open standard.
...

...
# Goose for VS Code
...
Bring [Goose](https://block.github.io/goose/)—the open-source, on-device AI agent—directly into your editor. Chat with Goose, reference your code, and let it handle full-stack engineering tasks without ever leaving VS Code.
...
Ask Goose questions about your code with full file context. Select code in your editor and send it to Goose with a single keystroke, or type `@` to search and attach any file from your workspace.
...
### Context Chips
...
Attach multiple files or code selections to your messages. Visual chips show exactly what context Goose sees, with support for both entire files and specific line ranges.
...
Pick up where you left
...
persist across VS Code
...
with full history—browse
...
sessions instantly.
...
See Goose think in real-time. Responses stream token-by-token with syntax-highlighted code blocks and one-click copy.
...
### From GitHub Releases
...
1. Download the `.vsix` from [Releases](https://github.com/block/vscode-goose/releases)
2. In VS Code: Extensions → `...` menu → Install from VSIX...
...

Title: Add selective extension loading for subagents to improve performance and security · Pull Request #4298 · aaif-goose/goose
URL: https://github.com/block/goose/pull/4298
Published: 2025-08-22T21:31:57.000Z
Author: tlongwell-block
Highlights:
- Repository: aaif-goose/goose | an open source, extensible AI agent that goes beyond code suggestions - install, execute, edit, and test with any LLM | 43K stars | Rust
...
## Add selective extension loading for subagents to improve performance and security
...
Implements selective MCP extension loading for subagents, allowing them to load only the extensions they need. This reduces startup time by 5-10x and memory usage by up to 80% for typical subagent tasks.
...
Added an optional `extension_filter` parameter to dynamic task creation that allows fine-grained control over which extensions subagents load:
...
- **Three filter modes**:
...
- `include`: Load only specified extensions
 - `exclude`: Load all except specified extensions 
 - `none`: Load no extensions (for pure computation tasks)
...
- **Fully backward compatible**: Existing code works without modification (defaults to loading all extensions)
- **Smart error handling**: Invalid extension names produce helpful error messages with available options
- **CLI visibility**: Extension filters are displayed in tool output for debugging
...
- Added `ExtensionFilter` enum with tagged serialization for clean JSON
- Updated `Task` struct with optional `extension_filter` field
- Modified `SubAgent::new()` to apply extension filtering logic
- Enhanced dynamic task tool schema to accept extension filter parameter
- Uses existing `normalize()` function for consistent extension name handling
- Added comprehensive test coverage
...
- **No filter (default)**: Same performance as before
- **Include mode (1-2 extensions)**: 5-10x faster startup, 80% memory reduction
- **None mode**: Near-instant startup with minimal memory footprint
...
By limiting subagent extensions, you can reduce the attack surface when processing untrusted content. For example, a subagent analyzing potentially malicious text can be restricted to the minimal required extensions, preventing it from accessing the filesystem, network, or other system resources even if prompt injection occurs. This provides a lightweight sandboxing mechanism for handling untrusted inputs.
...
```bash
# LLM can now optimize subagent performance:
"Create a subagent with only the developer extension to run shell commands"
"Create a subagent with no extensions to process data"
"Create a subagent excluding the github extension"
```

Title: fix: preserve unparseable extension entries during config refresh
URL: https://github.com/aaif-goose/goose/pull/9439
Published: N/A
Author: N/A
Highlights:
- State: merged
- Author: mvanhorn
- Created: 2026-05-27T05:18:13Z
- Updated: 2026-06-12T06:21:19Z
- Repository: aaif-goose/goose
- Number: #9439
- +389 -37 in 1 files
- Merged: 2026-06-11T05:24:47Z
- Merge commit: c6a3b5b4e17d382305fdb0e1cc2da6904784473f
...
Preserves unparseable extension entries during config refresh so a single malformed entry no longer wipes its siblings from the on-disk config when a user enables/disables/removes a different extension.
...
Issue #8970 reported that toggling one extension via the UI silently dropped a different, unrelated extension entry whose YAML was malformed. The old `set_param(EXTENSIONS_CONFIG_KEY, &extensions)` serialized the parsed map back to disk, which by construction excluded any entry that failed `serde_yaml::from_value:: `. Result: the user lost extension config they did not even touch in that session.
...
- `parse_extensions_map` extracted from `get_extensions_map_with_config`; it logs the offending key as `info!` (not `warn!`) and skips the entry instead of dropping it from the raw mapping
- New `with_raw_extensions_mapping` wrapper performs mutations against the raw `serde_yaml::Mapping`, then writes only the keys it actually touched -- so untouched-but-unparseable siblings round-trip unchanged
- `save_extensions_map` replaced by callers that go through the wrapper
- Six new tests covering: clean-sibling preservation, unparseable-sibling preservation across set_extension_enabled, set_extension add, remove_extension, single-log-per-malformed-key, and existing platform-filter behavior
...
`cargo test -p goose --lib config::extensions::` -- 6/6 pass.
...
970
...
> Much appreciated, @michaelneale. Preserving unparseable extension entries through config refresh prevents silent data loss.
